titleStmt.title: Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi collection
Miracles of Mary: ʾAkko-nu bǝʾsi collection

publicationStmt.publisher: Die Schriftkultur des christlichen Äthiopiens und Eritreas: Eine multimediale Forschungsumgebung / Beta maṣāḥǝft

availability.licence: This file is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0.

encodingDesc.p: Encoded according to the Beta maṣāḥǝft Guidelines. These Guidelines detail the TEI format ruled by the Beta maṣāḥǝft Schema. The present TEI file is enriched with an Xquery transformation taking advantage of the exist-db database instance where the data is stored and of the many external resources to which this data points to.

citeStructure.unit: Miracle of Sophia, abbess of Mount Carmel
Miracle of three Arabs in Rīf and Salomon, abbot of Kalamon
Miracle of a picture of Mary speaking to a worker
Miracle of a rich man from Qʷalāsis who fought the Qʷǝz and whose eye was pierced by an arrow
Miracle of the Bishop of Rome, ʾAbbās (Leo)
Miracle of the thirsty dog whom the Virgin gave water
Miracle of George the Younger, who was delivered from prison
Miracle of a widow and her poor daughters Maria, Martha, and Yawāhit
Miracle of the old priest Qaṭīr at ʾƎlkǝsus (al-Khusus)
Miracle of Anastasius, a deacon of Rome
Miracle of a European man with a petrified foot
Miracle of the two scribes who were brothers
Miracle of a French painter whose scaffold was overthrown by the devil
Miracle of the preservation of the Church of the Virgin in ʾAṯrīb
Miracle of a woman from Hartalom/Hartarom with broken legs
Miracle of St Mary on Yǝsḥaq
Miracle of a sick man in a hospital whom Mary carries to Jerusalem
Miracle of the child girl called Mary, from Dafrā
Introduction to the Miracles of Mary
Miracle of the construction of a church in Jericho
Miracle of the two brothers Tāg and Nazīb from Dalgā
Miracle of the scribe Damianus from Philippi
Miracle of the Phoenician evil man called Bārok
Miracle of Abraham, his wife Gerā and their blind daughter Elisabeth from Badramān
Miracle of Juliana and Barbara from Bethlehem
Miracle of a Jew of ʾAḫmīm
Miracle of a poor widow and her son who became a thief and was crucified
Miracle of the blind priest John from the church of St Mercurius in Cairo
Miracle of the youth Zechariah, from Rome
Miracle of a monk of the Monastery of St Samuel of Kalamon
The Story of a Cannibal of Qǝmr
Miracle of the leprous Bishop Mercurius
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a poor monk whom Mary gave a garment

abstract.p: A collection of miracles of Mary which is fixed in its content and sequence in its manuscript transmission: after the introduction to the Miracles follow a fixed sequence of 33 miracles, each of is followed by a stanza of the ʾakko-nu bǝʾsi hymn, which might be followed by a miniature illustrating the miracle.

calendar.p: ʿĀmata ʿālam/ʿĀmata ʾəm-fəṭrat (Era of the World)
ʿĀmata śəggāwe (Era of the Incarnation – Ethiopian)

revisionDesc.change: Added elements on the poor monk who begs the Virgin for garment and food.

bibl.title: Aethiopische Marienhymnen
Abhandlungen der Sächsischen Akademie der Wissenschaften

series.t:title: Abhandlungen der Sächsischen Akademie der Wissenschaften
series.t:biblScope: 68, Abhandlungen der philologisch-historischen Klasse der Sächsischen Akademie der Wissenschaften, 33/4

bibl.biblScope: 68, Abhandlungen der philologisch-historischen Klasse der Sächsischen Akademie der Wissenschaften, 33/4

div.label: Introduction to the Miracles of Mary
Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a poor monk whom Mary gave a garment
div.note: The narration about the poor monk does not appear in most manuscripts but only occurs as the first (sometimes as the second) narration in a few manuscripts.

div.label: Miracle of a picture of Mary speaking to a worker

label.ref: Miracle of Mary: Miracle of a picture of Mary speaking to a worker
